title: Verific Frontend Blasts Memories to DFFs
body:
I've been using this simple memory as a test case: [test_mem.tar.gz](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/1843592/test_mem.tar.gz). The memory in examples/smtbmc/demo3.v works as well but is much larger, so it is slow to run.

With the normal front-end, memories are preserved when run as follows:
```yosys -p "proc; memory -nomap; opt; prep -top test_mem -nordff; write_smt2 test_mem.smt2" -ql test_mem.log test_mem.v```

In this case, test_mem.smt2 contains Arrays.

With the Verific frontend, the memory is transformed into a bunch of DFFs. 
```yosys -p "verific -vlog2k test_mem.v; verific -import -v test_mem; proc; memory -nomap; opt; prep -top test_mem -nordff; write_smt2 verific_test_mem.smt2" -ql verific_test_mem.log```

Here, verific_test_mem.smt2 does not contain Arrays.

It appears that \mem is imported as a NetBus instead of a RamNet, which is checked for in frontends/verific.cc. Additionally, there is an instance with Type OPER_DUAL_PORT_RAM which is unhandled. I tried adding a RTLIL::Memory in that case, but got stuck trying to connect it properly.

Here's the log file for the verific case: [verific_test_mem.log](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/1843573/verific_test_mem.log)
code_blocks:
In this case, test_mem.smt2 contains Arrays.

With the Verific frontend, the memory is transformed into a bunch of DFFs.
